display.purpose: Ceremonial setup titles only; oversized tracked uppercase context.

body-reading.purpose: Default explanatory copy tuned for constrained line length.

label-dense-cap.purpose: Top HUD identity row and compact headings.

shadows.name: active-player-ring
shadows.purpose: Active turn emphasis on roster chips layered over gold borders.

motion.name: ease-out-expo-setup
motion.purpose: Preferred calm easing echoed from Setup interactive tokens.

motion.name: topbar-intro-legacy-ease
motion.purpose: Historical overshoot easing on TOP_BAR intro; refactor toward expo per PRODUCT atmosphere.

components.name: Midnight HUD Panel
components.description: Default chrome container for explanatory panels atop the abyss canvas.
components.css: .ds-panel { background: #16213e; color: #eeeeee; border-radius: 8px; padding: 16px; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-panel__text { margin: 0; line-height: 1.45; font-size: 1rem; }

components.name: Overlay Sheet
components.html:
Select reward

Short supporting line.
components.css: .ds-overlay-sheet { background: #16213e; border-radius: 12px; padding: 32px; color: #eeeeee; max-width: min(720px, 90vw); } .ds-overlay-sheet__title { font-size: 1.5rem; margin: 0 0 12px; font-weight: 600; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-overlay-sheet__body { margin: 0; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.45; opacity: 0.85; }

components.name: Selectable Reward Tile
components.description: Card-sized affordance borrowing reward grid cues.
components.css: .ds-reward-option { background: #0f0f23; color: #eeeeee; border: 2px solid #333; border-radius: 8px; padding: 16px; font-size: 1rem; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 0.15s ease-out, border-color 0.15s ease-out, background 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-reward-option:hover { border-color: #f39c12; background: #1a1a3e; transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-reward-option:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f39c12; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: HUD Top Strip
components.description: Condensed translucent header band over the battlefield.
components.css: .ds-top-strip { display: flex; align-items: center; height: 36px; padding: 0 16px; background: rgba(10, 10, 25, 0.9); color: #fff; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-top-strip__hero { font-size: 0.85rem; font-weight: 600; text-transform: capitalize; }

components.name: Active Player Bubble
components.description: Multiplayer roster active state echoes gold ring plus glow cues.
components.css: .ds-player-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 6px 8px; border-radius: 6px; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1); background: rgba(10,10,25,0.6); font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; color: #ffffff; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-player-chip--active { border: 2px solid #f1c40f; box-shadow: 0 0 12px rgba(241,196,15,0.6); background: rgba(241,196,15,0.1); }

narrative.overview:
The client currently reads as **two coordinated families** rather than one flat token bundle. Global play chrome (shell, hex canvas, reusable panels, many overlays, top bar, player bubbles) favors **cold midnight blues** and **neutral system sans** tuned for hierarchy of game state over decoration. Dedicated flows (especially setup, parchment-style rewards and level-ups, combat CSS notes and Pixi atmosphere) deliberately adopt **muted brass, earth borders, antiqued parchment cream text** so menu moments feel grounded and map-table-ish without drifting into flashy fantasy UI.

PRODUCT.md asks for clarity before spectacle and rejects dominant mystical cliché plus heroic bombast. This dual track is pragmatic today: overlays already document their intent in CSS comments (setup aligned with overlays; combat palette aligned with Pixi atmospherics). Going forward the goal is to **narrow drift**: promote shared primitives (radii tier, tonal borders, eased motion vocabulary) upward so new screens inherit one spine even when palettes branch.

The system rejects **looks-over-scanability** chrome: dense stat rows, multi-panel combat, solo cognitive load demand consistent hit targets and readable captions over flair.
keyCharacteristics: Midnight **void-indigo / canvas-abyss** pair frames the Pixi hex board and many cards.
**Parchment-earth** overlays provide warm episodic containment without implying mystic gimmickery.
**System UI sans** everywhere today: fast, predictable, slightly generic unless weight and letter-spacing cues mark titles.
**Stat accent colors** intentionally flat and categorical (purple fame track legacy, teal only in debug-heavy replay tooling).
**Motion** is uneven: tactical dimming filters on `body`/`app`; some intros use overshoot easing that should converge on calm expo easing for PRODUCT alignment.

rules.name: The Two-Family Rule
rules.body: When adding a panel, declare whether it belongs to **cold chrome** (void / abyss / midnight) or **parchment session** overlays. Avoid inventing a third decorative stack per feature.

rules.name: The Stat Color Semantics Rule
rules.body: Reuse categorical stat hues (`stat-level-amethyst`, `stat-fame-gold`, `stat-armor-slate`, `stat-influence-sky`) wherever matching hero identity labels to reinforce recognition; do not recolor arbitrarily per screen.

rules.name: The OKLCH Setup Lane Rule
rules.body: `.setup-screen` uses nested OKLCH custom properties documented in-component; migrating those tokens onto `:root` is preferred long-term while keeping authored OKLCH as canonical for that lane.

rules.name: The System Spine Rule
rules.body: Until a purposeful display face is introduced, retain the single stack everywhere; differentiation is spacing and weight rather than pairing.

rules.name: The Legibility Spine Rule
rules.body: Never set long instructional copy smaller than Body Reading tokens; PRODUCT solo clarity depends on deliberate reading comfort.

dos: **Do** reuse the **void → abyss → panel** tier ladder for new HUD unless a flow participates in parchment or combat palettes intentionally.
**Do** cite **OKLCH setup tokens** or promote them upward when refactoring rather than cloning approximate hex anew.
**Do** anchor combat-adjacent gold accents around **Antique Combat Gold** (#d4a574 family) documented alongside Pixi's illustrated layers.
**Do** preserve **readable flat hierarchy** inside dense flows (solo cognitive load mandate).
**Do** consolidate radii (`6px`, `8px`, `12px`) as the default scale before inventing orphan radii.
**Do** plan audio direction **ambient grounded map energy** complementary to subdued UI contrasts ( INTERFACE NOT THE HERO ).
**Do** implement future motion on **exponential ease-out curves** resembling setup's `--ease-out-expo`.
donts: **Don't** let **Dominant mystical fantasy cliché** (heavy rune shimmer, incense fog decorative noise) occlude authoritative engine-driven state readability.
**Don't** soundtrack or visually stage **Epic heroic bombast**: overwrought triumphant framing contradicts restrained crunch-forward mood.
**Don't** bury legibility under ornamental stacks that cause players to mistrust moves or LOS clarity (explicit PRODUCT anti-pattern on looks-over-scanability).

**Don't** sprinkle **neon candy accent rainbows** arbitrarily; limit categorical color to semantic stat roles (+ intentional debug telemetry hues like teal in replay tooling isolated from default play overlays).
**Don't** enlarge **glassmorphism + heavy blur layering** casually; tonal scrims suffice today.
**Don't** widen **thin side-stripe brand gimmicks (`border-left > 1px colored`)** purely for sparkle; impeccable shared prohibition.
